findings.problem: Once Sarah names the real injury with "You crawl into bed like they're fine," the back third keeps paraphrasing the same moral fact through multiple almost-confessions and internal summaries instead of converting that pressure into a cleaner action/decision beat. The chapter's ethical center is strong, but it gets re-proved often enough to read as thematic over-explanation rather than escalation.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress or differentiate the sequence spanning snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:393, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:399, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:455, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:500, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:522, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:542. Keep the must-land beats: Sarah forces physical separation, Nate recognizes the bed/false-identity horror, Nate fails to confess. But let only one late private articulation carry the full diagnosis; every later paragraph should either impose a new cost or push him into the final non-confession.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised equivalent of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:393-556, the reader should encounter Sarah's accusation, one distinct private articulation of the bed/fraud truth, and one failed attempt to speak. Any additional paragraph in that stretch fails if it only restates the same moral realization without adding a new cost, a new fact, or a changed decision.

findings.problem: Sarah's confrontation repeatedly resolves into balanced, quotable thesis lines. The style bible gives her precision and forensic calm under pressure, but here too many peak lines arrive as perfectly shaped set-pieces, so the fight starts to sound composed rather than overheard.
findings.rewrite_direction: Roughen selected peaks at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:253, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:343, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:389, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:449, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:459. Keep Sarah specific and in control, but route some turns through concrete domestic detail, interruption, or minor self-correction instead of slogan-clean phrasing.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised equivalents of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:253-259, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:343-351, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:389-459, at least two of the current set-piece lines must be broken into messier speech or recast through concrete present-tense observation. Pass only if Sarah still sounds precise but no longer sounds like every turning-point line was written to stand alone as a maxim.

findings.problem: The chapter relies on the same sentence machinery to mark emotional turns: characters 'looked at him,' words 'landed hard/harder,' voices 'stayed' level/even, and lines 'came out' sharp or too close to the bone. In a confrontation this long, those repeated templates make the prose feel automated at the exact moments that should feel most freshly observed.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the beat reporting around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:159-189, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:253-275, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:333-343. Replace several of the current transition formulas with distinct physical business, sensory change, or relational detail so each leverage shift has its own texture.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised equivalents of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:159-189, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:253-275, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:333-343, no single emotional-transition template should appear more than twice. Pass only if at least three turn markers are conveyed through different concrete signals rather than another 'looked,' 'landed,' 'voice stayed,' or 'came out' construction.

findings.problem: In the bedroom aftermath, Nate's panic-guilt intermittently resolves into elegantly packaged insight instead of the simpler bodily register the style bible assigns him under acute fear. The intelligence is plausible; the polish is the issue. These lines read retrospectively authored rather than fully trapped inside the moment.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:455-526 so Nate's near-confession stays in pressure-register prose: more sensory memory, bodily recoil, and broken thought; less capsule phrasing and less explanatory compression of the time-travel mechanics. Keep the ethical horror explicit, but make it arrive through remembered contact and present physical dread.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised equivalent of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:455-526, Nate's fear and guilt should be legible primarily through concrete remembered touch, bodily sensation, and broken thought rather than polished summary. Pass only if at least one current capsule judgment is replaced by scene-based image or action that conveys the same moral fact without essay-like phrasing.

summary: FAIL across all four lenses. The chapter has the right confrontation engine, preserves continuity, matches the style bible's past-tense requirement, and does not show obvious sanitization drift or continuity contradiction. But it stays below pass-level because the back third over-proves the same ethical realization, Sarah's sharpest lines tip into over-composed quotability, and repeated prose templates plus polished late interiority make key pressure beats feel written rather than freshly lived.
